name: The Brandywine Pursuit Rules
text: Welcome to The Brandywine Pursuit, an unofficial fan Adventure Pack, developed by A Long-extended Party (ALeP). Escape the wolf-cult by white-water rafting down the Brandywine river in the third adventure pack of the Fell Summer cycle. Component Overview The Brandywine Pursuit Adventure Pack includes 70 cards, consisting of:  • 3 Rules Cards • 37 Encounter Cards  • 2 Hero Cards • 3 Quest Cards  • 24 Player Cards • 1 Player Objective Card Web Resources You can check for any updates from A Long-extended Party at www.alongextendedparty.com. You can build decks and track your plays of these scenarios and others online through RingsDB at www.ringsdb.com. For general rules and definitions of common game terms, visit the Quest Companion at bit.ly/2MKkak9.

name: The Brandywine Pursuit 1
text: Expansion Symbol The cards in the Fell Summer cycle can be identified by this symbol before each card’s collector number. The Brandywine Pursuit Difficulty level = 8 All was confusion and noise in the darkness. “We do not know how many of the cult are here,” said one of the heroes, “but if this is indeed their lair, there may be more than we guessed. And we must find some place to tend to Jack.” Another spoke. “I glimpsed a large raft further along the shore. We might take that and slip away down the Brandywine.” The first hero paused in thought. “Yes… though if these brigands give chase, we should draw them beyond the settled lands. To Sarn Ford, then.” They turned toward the raft, but one of their company cried out suddenly. “Look, there! A hobbit! He is fleeing the cultists.” Indeed, the hobbit—a Mr. Drogo Baggins, as they would soon learn—had seized his chance amid the turmoil. A

name: The Brandywine Pursuit 2
text: quiet evening walk along the river had gone awry when he stumbled upon two look‑outs clad in purple and was dragged to their camp to await their leader’s return. Now he was running as fast as his legs could carry him. He spied the strangers ahead and made a desperate dash toward them, only to halt, breathless, at the sight of the makeshift raft they were scrambling onto. The shouts of his rescuers grew urgent, and the cries of the ruffians behind him louder still. “I do not like adventures,” thought Drogo miserably. “I have had one unpleasant turn after another, all because I chose to enjoy an evening ramble about the Bridgefields and blundered into a camp of footpads.” “Come, quickly!” He looked again at the loosely‑tied logs bobbing upon the river. “It is just like the Buckland Ferry,” he told himself. “I step aboard, and off we go.” Yet his feet would not obey. A stone whistled past his ear and splashed into the water. “We cannot dally! Hop aboard!” Drogo jumped—and staggered as he landed. One of the Big Folk caught his arm before he toppled into the river. “Thank you, er, Madam.” The grim‑faced hero ignored him and hurried to help push the raft from the shore.

name: The Brandywine Pursuit 3
text: Drogo sat, thinking that safest, then sprang up again as guilt pricked him. Surely, he ought to be helping. The same woman seized him once more as he lurched toward the edge. “Dreadfully sorry,” he stammered. “I have little experience with boats. When I was courting Primula—that is, my wife, though she was not my wife then—” The hero fixed him with a stern look. “Sit. Somewhere away from the edge.” “Absolutely,” said Drogo, thoroughly abashed. “Very good idea.” He settled near the middle of the raft, though part of him still longed to be useful. His rescuers were already hard at work: tightening ropes, lashing makeshift paddles, and keeping wary eyes on the ruffians. The current tugged at the raft, urging it downstream. “The Brandywine Pursuit” is played with an encounter deck built with all the cards from the following encounter sets: The Brandywine Pursuit and Ruffians. (Ruffians can be found in The Shire’s Reckoning deluxe expansion.) These sets are indicated by the following icons:

name: The Brandywine Pursuit 4
text: The River Deck The River deck represents the shifting river currents that the players will need to face while escaping their pursuers. To build the River deck, take each location with the Main Current trait from The Brandywine Pursuit encounter set and shuffle them together. This is the River deck. Set the River deck near the quest deck. If a Main Current location leaves play without being explored, place it in the River deck discard pile. If the River deck is ever empty, shuffle the River deck discard pile back into the River deck. The echoes of battle still rang in their ears as the heroes gathered themselves. “We have won ourselves a little peace,” one said. “We must deal with this cult in due time, but for now we need rest and counsel. Wolfsbane, let me see that wound. I will clean it, and I have herbs that may ease the hurt.” They debated until the grey light of dawn crept over the river, and then slept as they could. The weather turned foul. Their plan was for a few to escort Drogo safely home, while the rest made for the Mathom House.

name: The Brandywine Pursuit 5
text: One spoke quietly. “We must go swiftly and in silence, and keep clear of the hobbit villages. Trouble dogs our heels, and I would not bring it upon the Little Folk.” “Agreed,” said the others. They shouldered their packs and stretched their weary, chilled limbs. Then all froze, for an awful howl rose in the distance. “There is trouble, right enough,” murmured one. “Onward, let us find the answers we seek.” The story continues in “Flight to Michel Delving”, the fourth Adventure Pack in the “Fell Summer” cycle.

name: Ghân-buri-Ghân
text: Planning Action: Search the top 10 cards of the encounter deck for a location, reveal it, and shuffle the encounter deck. Then, search the top 10 cards of your deck for a [lore] attachment, play it for no cost, and shuffle your deck. (Limit once per round.)

name: Ose-bori-Ose
text: Response: After you play a [lore] attachment, if it is not attached to a character, your threat is considered to be 20 until the end of the round.

name: Sharp-eyed Drû
text: Sharp-eyed Drû gets +1 [defense] while your threat is 20 or less, and +1 [defense] while the active location has at least 4 [threat]. If both are true, Sharp-eyed Drû also gains sentinel.

name: Mushroom Gatherer
text: Refresh Action: Exhaust Mushroom Gatherer to either deal 1 damage to any 1 enemy in play, or heal 1 damage from any 1 character in play.

name: Drû-buri-Drû
text: Response: At the end of any phase, exhaust Drû-buri-Drû to move a [lore] card attached to an encounter card to another eligible encounter card.

name: Drúedain Seer
text: Response: After Drúedain Seer commits to the quest, place 1 [threat] token on any 1 location in the staging area to ready any 1 Wose or Scout character.

name: Gorgûn-hunter
text: Gorgûn-hunter gets +1 [attack] while your threat is 20 or less, and +1 [attack] while the active location has at least 4 [threat]. If both are true, Gorgûn-hunter also gains ranged.

name: Poisoned Darts
text: Play Poisoned Darts into the staging area unattached. If unattached, attach Poisoned Darts to the next eligible enemy that enters the staging area. Response: When the attached enemy would take any amount of damage, increase that amount by 1.

name: Púkel-man
text: Attach to a location or enemy. Attached card gets +1 [threat]. Response: At the end of the round, place 1 progress on attached location or deal 1 damage to attached enemy.

name: Chieftain’s Staff
text: Attach to a Wose hero. While the active location has at least 4 [threat], Wose characters you control get +1 [willpower]. Planning Action: Exhaust Chieftain’s Staff and place 1 [threat] token on any 1 location in the staging area to reduce the cost of the next [lore] card you play this phase by 1.

name: Masters of the Forest
text: Setup: Each player that controls at least 1 hero with the Woodman, Ranger, or Wose traits may opt in by raising their threat by 2 plus an additional 2 for each hero they control that does not have one of those traits. Each player that opted in draws 1 card for each other player that opted in. Flip this card over.

name: Masters of the Forest
text: Players who opted in to this objective and cards they control ignore the following text on non-unique locations and enemies:  • “immune to player card effects”  • “cannot have attachments”  • “cannot have player card attachments”  • “cannot have non-X attachments” (where X  is a Trait or card type).

name: Makeshift Raft
text: You cannot have more than X total characters, Item cards, and Mount cards in play under your control. X is the number of resource tokens on Makeshift Raft. Response: After a Brigand enemy engaged with you is destroyed, place 2 resource tokens on Makeshift Raft. (Limit once per round.) Forced: At the end of the round, remove 1 resource token from Makeshift Raft (2 resource tokens instead if an active location was explored this round).

name: Drogo Baggins
text: Response: Flip Drogo Baggins and add him to the staging area to cancel the effects of a location just revealed from the River deck. Shuffle that card back into the River deck and reveal another card from the River deck, immediately traveling to it. If Drogo Baggins leaves play, the players lose the game.

name: Drogo Baggins
text: Refresh Action: Exhaust a character you control to flip Drogo Baggins and take control of him. Any player may trigger this effect. Forced: At the end of the round, deal 1 damage to Drogo Baggins. If Drogo Baggins leaves play, the players lose the game.

name: Sarn Ford
text: Shadow cards are immune to player card effects. Forced: When a card effect would instruct a player to remove any amount of resource tokens from a Raft objective they control, that player instead assigns the same amount of damage to a hero they control.

name: Girdley Island Shores
text: Immune to card effects. Response: When Girdley Island Shores is explored, each player either places 2 resource tokens on a Raft objective they control, or puts into play from their hand an ally with a printed cost 3 or lower.

name: Buckland Waterfalls
text: While Buckland Waterfalls is the active location, Brigand enemies get –50 engagement cost. When Revealed: Discard cards from the encounter deck until 1 Brigand enemy is discarded (2 Brigand enemies instead if there are 3 or more players in the game). Add each discarded Brigand enemy to the staging area.

name: Congested Detour
text: While Congested Detour is the active location, the current quest gains battle. When Revealed: Each player either exhausts a ready character they control, or raises their threat by 5.

name: Dangerous Rapids
text: When Revealed: Each player discards 1 random card from their hand. Forced: After the players commit characters to the quest, either Dangerous Rapids contributes its [threat] to the staging area until the end of the phase, or discard cards from the encounter deck until a treachery is discarded and resolve the “when revealed” effect on that treachery.

name: Narrow Gorge
text: While Narrow Gorge is the active location, characters lose the sentinel and ranged keywords. When Revealed: Each player either exhausts a ready character they control, or deals 3 damage among characters they control.

name: Swampy Passage
text: While Swampy Passage is the active location, Creature enemies cannot take damage. When Revealed: Discard cards from the encounter deck until 1 Creature enemy is discarded (2 Creature enemies instead if there are 3 or more players in the game). Add each discarded Creature enemy to the staging area.

name: Tumultuous Waters
text: When Revealed: Reveal 1 encounter card (2 encounter cards instead if there are 3 or more players in the game). Forced: After a character commits to the quest, either deal 1 damage to that character or it gets –1 [willpower] until the end of the phase.

name: Unmapped Falls
text: While Unmapped Falls is the active location, the current quest gains siege. When Revealed: Each player discards an ally, Item or Mount card they control.

name: White Water
text: When Revealed: Remove 1 resource token from each Raft objective in play. Forced: At the beginning of the quest phase, remove 1 resource token from each Raft objective in play.

name: Agile Assailant
text: Agile Assailant cannot take combat damage while being attacked by fewer than 3 characters.
shadow: Shadow: If the attacking enemy is still in play at the end of the combat phase, remove 1 resource token from a Raft objective you control.

name: Brawny Outlaw
text: Forced: For each excess point of combat damage dealt by Brawny Outlaw, remove 1 resource token from a Raft objective you control.
shadow: Shadow: For each excess point of damage dealt by this attack, remove 1 resource token from a Raft objective you control.

name: Torch Wielder
text: Forced: At the end of the round, if Torch Wielder is engaged with you, remove 1 resource token from a Raft objective you control.
shadow: Shadow: Either discard a character you control, or remove 1 resource token from a Raft objective you control.

name: Overbourn Adder
text: Cannot be dealt shadow cards. Forced: After Overbourn Adder attacks, discard an Item attachment from the defending character. If you cannot, discard the defending character instead.

name: Withywindle Rats
text: Cannot be dealt shadow cards. Withywindle Rats makes 2 additional attacks against the engaged player during each combat phase.
shadow: Shadow: Deal 1 damage to the attacking enemy and 1 damage to the defending character.

name: Zealous Beaver
text: Cannot be dealt shadow cards. Players cannot declare defenders against Zealous Beaver. Forced: When Zealous Beaver deals any amount of damage to a character you control, cancel that damage and remove X resource tokens from a Raft objective you control instead. X is the amount of damage just canceled.

name: Sandbank
text: Sandbank cannot become the active location. While Sandbank is in the staging area, progress must be placed on Sandbank before it can be placed on the active location. Quest Action: Exhaust a character you control to place X progress on this location, where X is the exhausted character’s [attack]. Any player may trigger this effect.

name: Enraged Swans
text: When Revealed: Either deal 1 damage to each character committed to the quest, or progress cannot be placed on locations until the end of the round.
shadow: Shadow: Until the end of the round, if any damage is dealt to the attacking enemy, an equal amount of resource tokens must be removed from a Raft objective you control.

name: Strong Current
text: When Revealed: Until the end of the phase, add 3 [threat] to the total [threat] in the staging area. Move the active location to the bottom of the River deck. Reveal and immediately travel to the top card of the River deck.
shadow: Shadow: Either remove X resource tokens from a Raft objective you control, or attacking enemy gets +X [attack]. X is the active location’s [threat].

name: Submerged Rocks
text: When Revealed: Each player either removes 2 resource tokens from a Raft objective they control, or discards a character they control.
shadow: Shadow: Remove 2 resource tokens from a Raft objective you control and discard the attacking enemy.

name: Unravelling Ropes
text: Forced: At the end of the round, remove 2 resource tokens from each Raft objective in play. Response: When this quest is defeated, each player places 2 resource tokens on a Raft objective they control.

name: Down the Brandywine
text: Setup: Each player takes control of 1 copy of Makeshift Raft and places 15 resource tokens on it. The first player takes control of Drogo Baggins (Boating side faceup). Set Sarn Ford aside, out of play. Make Girdley Island Shores the active location. Create the River deck (see rules) and set it next to the quest deck. Shuffle the encounter deck and discard cards until 1[pp] Brigand enemies are discarded. Add each discarded Brigand enemy to the staging area.

name: Down the Brandywine
text: Forced: After the active location is explored, reveal the top card of the River deck and immediately travel to it. Forced: At the end of the quest phase, if no active location was explored this round, each player discards 1 card from the encounter deck. Add each Brigand enemy discarded this way to the staging area. This stage cannot be defeated unless there are at least 3 locations in the victory display.

name: We Must Keep Moving
text: When Revealed: Distribute 2 resource tokens among Raft objectives in play. Shuffle the encounter discard pile into the encounter deck. Discard cards from the encounter deck until 1[pp] Brigand enemies are discarded. Add each discarded Brigand enemy to the staging area.

name: We Must Keep Moving
text: Forced: After the active location is explored, reveal the top card of the River deck and immediately travel to it. Forced: At the end of the quest phase, if no active location was explored this round, each player discards 1 card from the encounter deck. Add each Brigand enemy discarded this way to the staging area. This stage cannot be defeated unless there are at least 6 locations in the victory display.

name: Battle at Sarn Ford
text: When Revealed: End the current phase. Remove each Raft objective from the game and discard each encounter card from play. For each engaged Brigand enemy discarded this way, the engaged player assigns 1 damage among characters they control. Make Sarn Ford the active location. Shuffle the encounter discard pile and any Brigand enemy in the victory display into the encounter deck. Each player discards cards from the encounter deck until they discard a Brigand enemy and puts it into play engaged with them, repeating this process until each player has 3 Brigand enemies engaged with them.

name: Battle at Sarn Ford
text: Skip the resource, planning, quest, and travel phases. If the encounter deck is empty at any time, shuffle the encounter discard pile back into the encounter deck. If there are no enemies in play, the players win the game.
